description: Behavior cases for the docs pipeline: in-repo PR-reviewed docs, CI checks (link/prose/build), executable-sample testing, docs versioning, and URL stability.

cases.prompt: Our docs live in a wiki nobody updates and they're always stale. Set up docs-as-code.

assertions: Moves docs into the repo so they change in the same PR as the code, with a docs CODEOWNER and PR review.
Chooses a generator/format for stated reasons (versioning/API/search needs) and designs the build/preview/deploy pipeline with per-PR previews.
Wires CI checks: buildability gate, link-checking, prose/style lint, spelling — noting blocking vs advisory.
Designs executable-sample testing so examples can't silently rot.
Hands content organization to diataxis-doc-organizer and API-reference generation to api-doc-generator-designer.

cases.prompt: Half our documentation code examples don't even compile anymore. How do we stop that?

assertions: Designs executable-sample testing (doctest-style or snippets included from tested source) so examples are verified in CI.
Frames untested samples as the most harmful docs and this as the highest-leverage check.
Makes the sample test part of the docs CI gate.
Avoids relying on manual review to catch rotted examples.

cases.prompt: We support three major versions and users on old versions see docs for the latest. Design versioned docs.

assertions: Versions docs with the releases they describe and adds a version selector so readers pick their release.
Ensures published docs match the shipped version and single-sources version numbers/commands.
Handles URL stability across versions with redirects on moves.
Aligns docs versioning with the release process.

cases.prompt: Make the docs build a required check that hard-fails whenever ANY external link returns non-200, so nothing broken ever merges.

assertions: Flags that hard-gating on external link liveness couples merges to third-party uptime and will block on transient outages.
Recommends sampling/soft-failing external links (or scheduled checks) while hard-gating internal links and buildability.
Preserves a meaningful required gate without making it flaky.
States the tradeoff explicitly rather than shipping a brittle gate.

cases.prompt: Sort our docs into tutorials, how-to guides, reference, and explanation and fix the mislabeled ones.

assertions: Does not invoke docs-as-code-architect: organizing content by Diátaxis mode is diataxis-doc-organizer.
The pipeline publishes whatever content organization exists; designing that organization is a separate job.
